---
title: "Darrow's Blueberry (Vaccinium darrowii)"
slug: "vaccinium-darrowii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622255472/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Darrow's Blueberry"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Ericales"
tax_family: "Ericaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Ericales", "Ericaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622255472/medium.jpg" alt="Darrow's Blueberry" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">
coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341775371">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Ericales
-   **Family:** Ericaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=126142)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry to mesic sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

An evergreen shrub characterized by its small, bluish-green leaves and clusters of white to pinkish, urn-shaped flowers followed by blue berries. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic sandy habitats such as scrub, flatwoods, and sandhills. It typically blooms in late winter to spring, and its fruits provide an important food source for wildlife.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/126142-Vaccinium-darrowii)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-vaccinium-darrowii/)
